notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 2nd-round pick (#60) to Chicago Bears for WR DJ Moore and a 2026 5th-round pick. Previously traded 6th-round pick to NYJ, 7th-round pick to CLE. Acquired two 7th-rounders (NYJ via Codrington, DAL via Elam trade).
roster_context: New coaching staff (HC Joe Brady, DC Jim Leonhard, OC Pete Carmichael) after Sean McDermott fired following divisional round loss to Denver. Transitioning from 4-3 to 3-4 defensive scheme. Josh Allen remains elite but the team has massive salary cap challenges (~$8-10M over) and 20 pending UFAs. The DJ Moore trade addresses the WR1 hole, but the defense needs a near-complete overhaul at multiple positions to fit Leonhard's 3-4 scheme. New Highmark Stadium opens in 2026.

needs.explanation: The Bills' pass rush has been a critical weakness for multiple years. Joey Bosa (UFA) is not expected back, and AJ Epenesa (UFA) is likely gone too. Greg Rousseau is the only proven pass rusher under contract but may move to 5-tech DE in the 3-4. Michael Hoecht is returning from a torn Achilles and was suspended 6 games in 2025. The Bills failed to record a single sack on Bo Nix in their playoff loss. In Leonhard's 3-4, OLB edge rushers are essential, and Buffalo has no proven one on the roster.
needs.current_starter: Greg Rousseau (transitioning to 3-4 DE/EDGE hybrid), Michael Hoecht (Achilles recovery)
needs.depth: Landon Jackson (2nd-year), Javon Solomon (depth). Bosa and Epenesa both UFAs departing. No proven 3-4 OLB pass rusher on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if top EDGE falls, otherwise Round 2-3

needs.explanation: The switch to a 3-4 defense under Jim Leonhard elevates LB to a critical need. Matt Milano (UFA, $11M dead cap) and Shaq Thompson (UFA) are both departing free agents. Terrel Bernard is under contract but underperformed in 2025. Dorian Williams is a potential cut candidate ($3.6M savings). The 3-4 requires two quality ILBs, and Buffalo currently has only Bernard as a viable starter. ESPN's Jordan Reid mocked CJ Allen (Georgia) to the Bills at #26 specifically for this need.
needs.current_starter: Terrel Bernard (under contract), Dorian Williams (potential cut)
needs.depth: Jimmy Ciarlo (reserve/future), Keonta Jenkins. Milano and Thompson both departing. Very thin for a 3-4 scheme requiring 4 LBs.

needs.explanation: The 3-4 scheme requires a true nose tackle, and the Bills don't have one. DaQuan Jones (UFA), Larry Ogunjobi (UFA), and Jordan Phillips (UFA) are all departing. Ed Oliver is expected to stay and could play 5-tech DE. Deone Walker (2025 4th-round pick) showed flashes as a rookie but is unproven as a full-time starter. TJ Sanders has potential but needs major development. Multiple analysts have called nose tackle a 'massive need' for the new scheme.
needs.current_starter: Ed Oliver (under contract, transitioning to 3-4 DE), Deone Walker (2nd year)
needs.depth: TJ Sanders, DeWayne Carter, Phidarian Mathis (re-signed). Jones, Ogunjobi, Phillips all departing as UFAs.

needs.explanation: Cole Bishop (2024 2nd-round pick) is the only entrenched starter. Jordan Poyer (34, UFA) is departing. Taylor Rapp is a likely cap casualty ($3M savings) after a knee injury ended his 2025 in Week 7 and he was rated 84th of 88 safeties by PFF. Damar Hamlin is a UFA. Sam Franklin was re-signed but is primarily a special teams player. The safety unit ranked 22nd in PFF overall grade in 2025. Buffalo needs a legit starter opposite Bishop, particularly one who fits Leonhard's versatile scheme.
needs.depth: Sam Franklin (re-signed, 3 years, primarily ST), Jordan Hancock (2025 5th-rounder, raw). Poyer, Hamlin, Darnell Savage all UFAs departing.

needs.explanation: Both starting interior OL are departing in free agency. Center Connor McGovern told reporters he does not expect to return and the team has not contacted him. LG David Edwards is also a UFA expected to price himself out of Buffalo's range. The Bills re-signed Alec Anderson (swing OL) and have Sedrick Van Pran-Granger, but neither is a proven starter. O'Cyrus Torrence is the starting RG and is under contract. Buffalo needs at least one quality IOL starter, likely at center or LG.
needs.current_starter: O'Cyrus Torrence (RG, under contract). LG and C both vacant after Edwards and McGovern depart.
needs.depth: Alec Anderson (re-signed, depth), Sedrick Van Pran-Granger, Tylan Grable. Could pursue a veteran FA like Isaac Seumalo.

needs.explanation: Christian Benford (extended) is the CB1 and Maxwell Hairston (2025 1st-rounder) showed promise in 11 games before injuries. However, Tre'Davious White (UFA) and Dane Jackson are both departing or cheap depth. Taron Johnson's fit in the new 3-4 defense is uncertain (potential position change being discussed). The Bills may not deploy a traditional nickel CB under Leonhard. CB3/depth is a clear need, even if the top two are solid.
needs.depth: Taron Johnson (uncertain fit in new scheme), Cam Lewis (utility DB). White departing as UFA. Nickel CB coaching position eliminated.

needs.explanation: The DJ Moore trade (March 5, 2026) significantly addresses the WR1 hole that was previously the team's most glaring need. Moore reunites with HC Joe Brady from their Carolina days and gives Josh Allen a proven top target. Khalil Shakir (extended through 2029) is a quality slot option. However, the WR room beyond Moore and Shakir remains uncertain: Keon Coleman has major maturity/consistency concerns after a disappointing 2025, Joshua Palmer was limited by injuries, Curtis Samuel is a near-certain cut, and Tyrell Shavers tore his ACL in the playoffs. Still could use another WR via draft.
needs.current_starter: DJ Moore (acquired via trade), Khalil Shakir (slot), Keon Coleman (WR3, make-or-break year)
needs.depth: Joshua Palmer (injury concerns), Tyrell Shavers (ACL, likely out until late 2026), Curtis Samuel (expected cut). Moore trade changes WR from Tier 1 to Tier 3.

needs.explanation: Dawson Knox ($17.8M cap hit) is being asked to restructure his contract or face release. Beane has confirmed talks are ongoing. Even if Knox is cut, Dalton Kincaid is a talented receiving TE and Jackson Hawes showed promise as a rookie blocker. TE is not a premium need but losing Knox without replacement would thin the room, especially given Kincaid's injury history.
needs.current_starter: Dalton Kincaid, Dawson Knox (restructure or cut pending)
needs.depth: Jackson Hawes (2nd year). If Knox is released, Bills save ~$9.7M but need a veteran TE addition.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 5-7 or veteran FA if Knox is released

free_agency_impact: Acquired WR DJ Moore from Bears for 2nd-round pick (March 5) — WR drops from Tier 1 to Tier 3
Re-signed S Sam Franklin to 3-year deal — provides ST depth but not a starter
Re-signed OL Alec Anderson to 1-year deal — provides IOL depth/competition
Re-signed DT Phidarian Mathis to 1-year deal — DL depth
Restructured RT Spencer Brown's contract (saved $10.42M cap space) — RT not a need
Losing C Connor McGovern (UFA, does not expect to return) — creates IOL need
Losing LG David Edwards (UFA, expected to price out) — creates IOL need
Losing EDGE Joey Bosa (UFA) and AJ Epenesa (UFA) — increases EDGE need
Losing LB Matt Milano (UFA, $11M dead cap) and Shaq Thompson (UFA) — increases LB need
Losing S Jordan Poyer (UFA, age 34) and likely cutting Taylor Rapp — increases S need
Losing CB Tre'Davious White (UFA) — minor CB depth impact
Losing DT DaQuan Jones, Larry Ogunjobi, Jordan Phillips (all UFAs) — increases IDL/NT need
Curtis Samuel expected to be released ($6.1M cap savings) — minimal impact, underperformed
Dawson Knox contract talks ongoing ($17.8M cap hit) — could be cut or restructured
Fired HC Sean McDermott; promoted OC Joe Brady to HC. New DC Jim Leonhard (3-4 scheme).
scheme_fit_notes: Jim Leonhard's 3-4 defense fundamentally changes Buffalo's personnel needs. The scheme requires a true nose tackle (not on roster), two 3-4 DEs (Oliver/Rousseau can fill), four LBs including two OLB edge rushers and two ILBs. This makes EDGE and LB significantly more important than in the prior 4-3. Leonhard's defense emphasizes sending 5+ rushers frequently (Denver influence under Vance Joseph), making versatile pass rushers and blitz-capable LBs premium targets. On offense, Joe Brady's scheme values route separators and YAC receivers over contested-catch big bodies, which explains the DJ Moore trade and why Keon Coleman's skill set may not be ideal. Safety versatility is critical in Leonhard's system — he praised Cole Bishop's ability to play multiple roles.
